entries.content: Decided to migrate the backend from Node.js to Rust due to clear performance bottlenecks.
Migrated storage layer from RocksDB to redb for simpler compilation and pure Rust.
Chose Tantivy for full-text search — BM25 scoring is far better than naive keyword matching.
API layer uses gRPC instead of REST, needing strong type contracts and streaming support.
Index loading switched to mmap + lazy page faults, projected memory down to 200MB.
Selected DashScope text-embedding-v4 as the semantic backend, 1024-dimension vectors with great quality.
Adopted jieba tokenization for Chinese text support, abandoning the whitespace-only approach.
Abandoned HNSW index in favor of IVF coarse quantization pre-filtering to boost search speed.
Monitoring stack: Prometheus + Grafana, logging via Loki.
Serialization uses bincode instead of JSON for better performance and smaller payloads.
User prefers dark-themed IDEs and finds bright backgrounds harsh on the eyes.
User is a Vim power user with 10 years of experience.
User favors functional programming style and avoids excessive mutable state.
User strongly prefers Rust, believing its type system offers the best safety guarantees.
User dislikes the Java ecosystem because installing a JVM is too heavyweight.
User starts work at 8 AM every morning, spending 30 minutes reading tech blogs first.
User leans toward writing tests before implementation, following TDD methodology.
User thinks microservices architecture is over-engineering — a monolith suffices for most projects.
User prefers minimalist CLI tools over GUIs, finding them more efficient.
User prefers PostgreSQL over MySQL due to its richer feature set.
HIPPMEM is a native Rust associative memory engine providing long-term memory for AI agents.
The project started in 2025, with the core idea of discovering associations at write time rather than assembling them at query time.
hippmem uses redb for embedded storage, avoiding external database dependencies.
The engine supports 14 associative edge types including entity overlap, topic overlap, causal, and more.
The spreading activation algorithm is based on Collins & Loftus' semantic network model.
Supports three retrieval modes: Fast, Balanced, and Deep.
Memory evolution uses Hebbian learning + temporal decay + summarization compression.
Full-text index is based on Tantivy, with Chinese tokenization via jieba-rs.
Embedding backends support DashScope, OpenAI-compatible, and deterministic fallback.
The engine works fully offline by default with no network dependencies.
Production deployment triggered OOM — memory spiked to 8GB causing Pod restarts.
After deploying the lazy load scheme, startup memory stabilized at 180MB and OOM disappeared.
V3 bugfix milestone completed — all three defects fixed, CI fully green.
Discovered HNSW index searches slow down at million-scale data, root cause is excessive distance computations.
By adding IVF coarse quantization pre-filtering, HNSW search speed improved by 5x.
Chinese tokenization previously only handled whitespace splitting — integrated jieba to solve Chinese entity extraction.
Manual testing confirmed the fallback backend has insufficient semantic precision — decided to introduce an embedding backend system.
The user is Alex, a senior software engineer working in tech.
In hindsight, choosing Rust was the right call — compile-time checks prevent countless runtime bugs.
User feels the complexity of distributed systems is often underestimated — avoid distribution when a monolith can handle it.
Previously stated the engine doesn't support Chinese, but it actually does via jieba tokenization.
Earlier we used REST API, but later realized gRPC is superior — corrected the architecture decision.
Initially claimed the BM25 channel needed no calibration, but testing revealed unbounded scores.
First thought HNSW alone was sufficient, later found that million-scale data requires IVF pre-filtering.
Previously preferred MySQL, but after migrating to PostgreSQL discovered window functions and JSONB are much better.
Nice weather today, perfect for a walk outside.
Had chicken teriyaki for lunch, pretty good.
The subway line B was delayed by 15 minutes again today.
The cat knocked a water glass off the table, shattered everywhere.
Planning to go hiking this weekend, hope the weather holds up.
